title: Make status capsule TAR extraction explicit for Python 3.14
goal: Replace the implicit tarfile.extractall behavior in the Bureau status capsule with an explicit, version-stable extraction filter while preserving or strengthening the existing traversal, link and special-entry refusal contract.

acceptance.assertion: The Python 3.12/3.14 tarfile extraction warning is reproduced by a focused status-capsule test before the change and absent after the explicit filter policy is applied.
_safe_extract passes an explicit tarfile filter or equivalent member policy whose behavior is documented and stable across supported Python versions.
Tests prove that path traversal, symbolic links, hard links and non-file/non-directory archive entries remain fail-closed; normal files and directories still extract.
The implementation resolves the API contract rather than suppressing DeprecationWarning globally or weakening tests.
Focused status-capsule tests and the complete Bureau validation suite pass on all supported CI Python versions.

metadata.source: BUR-2026-005-T018 closeout review and Bureau PR #496 Python 3.12 CI
metadata.source_observation: GitHub run 29245925498, validate (3.12), emitted 19 DeprecationWarning instances at src/bureau/status_capsule.py:283 because handle.extractall(destination) does not provide an explicit filter before Python 3.14 changes the default.
existing_safety_checks: resolved target remains below destination root
does_not_establish: need to support untrusted arbitrary archives

lease_scope_migration.rationale: The registered affected code is exactly status_capsule.py::_safe_extract.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks

metadata.outcome: Bureau PR #789 made status-capsule TAR extraction version-stable by passing an explicit fully_trusted tarfile filter after the existing fail-closed member validation. The exact implementation head passed local full validation and GitHub Python 3.10/3.12 plus CodeQL, was merged, and is present on main.
validation: Exact implementation head f4671f439cf5cd8ac260197e8ec75e812a7791d9: local make validate passed with Ruff, 785 tests, systemkatalog boundary and bureau check valid: True.
GitHub PR #789 validate (3.10) passed on the exact implementation head.
GitHub PR #789 validate (3.12) passed on the exact implementation head.
GitHub PR #789 CodeQL actions, CodeQL python and aggregate CodeQL all passed on the exact implementation head.

The focused regression test escalates DeprecationWarning to an error around _safe_extract; its Python 3.12 CI pass proves the changed extraction call no longer emits the targeted warning on that supported CI runtime.
Post-merge readback shows origin/main at merge commit 0d6cb9d3e96a2ed89f3d437cb56681f2e00fdc5f and src/bureau/status_capsule.py contains handle.extractall(destination, filter="fully_trusted").
The merge-commit tree and exact implementation-head tree are identical at 8d6fc131e5265e289a6752bea5281f8800f6c110.

verified_acceptance.bur-2026-005-t020-live-warning: Before-change evidence is the task source observation from GitHub run 29245925498 with 19 DeprecationWarning instances at the implicit extractall call. After the change, the focused warning-as-error regression passed in the PR Python 3.12 validation job.
verified_acceptance.bur-2026-005-t020-explicit-filter: _safe_extract now passes filter="fully_trusted" explicitly. This preserves the prior extraction semantics while preventing Python 3.14 from changing behavior through a new default.
verified_acceptance.bur-2026-005-t020-security-invariants: Regression tests prove path traversal, symbolic links, hard links and FIFO/special entries remain fail-closed, while normal files and directories still extract.
verified_acceptance.bur-2026-005-t020-no-warning-suppression: No global or local warning suppression was introduced; the regression test instead turns DeprecationWarning into an error.
verified_acceptance.bur-2026-005-t020-full-validation: Local full validation passed and all supported CI Python matrix jobs (3.10 and 3.12) plus CodeQL passed on the exact implementation head.

tradeoffs: filter="fully_trusted" is intentionally safe here only because _safe_extract rejects traversal, links and special entries before extraction; that ordering is part of the security contract and must be revisited if member validation changes.

Python 3.14 was not a configured CI matrix runtime for this repository at closeout; the compatibility result is established by making the filter explicit and by passing every currently supported CI Python version, not by a direct Python 3.14 job.

verification.verification_note: PR #789 is merged as 0d6cb9d3e96a2ed89f3d437cb56681f2e00fdc5f; exact-head local validation, Python 3.10/3.12 CI and CodeQL passed; post-merge main contains the explicit filter and has the same tree as the reviewed implementation head.
